notes: Reviewed the fifth heuristic-only claim batch after routing-heuristic improvements.
Satisfied deterministic CLI/runtime claims only when the batch5 evidence bundle records direct command proof.
Kept ownership-boundary and bundled-skill behavior rules unknown unless the evidence directly proved the behavior.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Reviewed as an ownership-boundary statement about the evaluation-process seam. The statement is plausible, but satisfaction needs cross-surface alignment proof rather than one eval run.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Link this claim to the matching adapter contract, eval test implementation, and packet-level evaluate/proposal-normalization tests before marking it satisfied.
unresolvedQuestions: Which deterministic test should be the canonical proof that this seam owns exactly the checked-in case suite, adapter-runner invocation, and chained summary artifacts?
